title: LLM decoder survivor cost proxy
hypothesis: The prompt-stress survivor paths should be ranked by an explicit rough implementation-cost proxy before selecting one path for RTL/OpenROAD work.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Among exact-safe decoder survivor rows, which approximation family has the lowest relative softmax/probability-path implementation cost under a stated proxy model?
include: exact next-token and top-k quality gate
relative exp-versus-PWL, normalization, datapath-width, and probability-write proxy terms
blocked rows for q6/q4/fp8_e5m2 quality caveats
exclude: selecting a final decoder probability format
follow_on_broad_sweep: implement the selected path as a bounded hardware-cost candidate
carry q8 PWL and bf16 PWL as the immediate frontier if the proxy is close

expected_benefit: turns quality-only survivor evidence into a concrete implementation-priority list
keeps the cost assumptions explicit and reviewable
prevents choosing fp16/bf16 probability storage alone when it preserves quality but leaves exact exp cost mostly intact
risks: the cost proxy is heuristic and not a replacement for RTL/OpenROAD evidence
PWL normalization cost weights may change once the block is physically implemented
the ranking still depends on the tiny decoder prompt-stress sweep
